dev_ecosystem.integrations_note: Counted from ordinals.com ecosystem page + UniSat wallet integration list + OKX Web3 BRC-20 marketplace listing (publicly accessible as of 2025-07-10): includes wallets (UniSat, Xverse, Leather, OKX Web3, Magic Eden wallet), marketplaces (Magic Eden, OKX NFT, Ordinals Market, Gamma.io, Ordswap), bridges/wrapping (ALEX Lab, Bitmap.game, Merlin Chain), CEX listings (Binance, OKX, Bybit, Coinbase, Kraken, Gate, HTX, KuCoin, MEXC, Bitget ~10 major), DeFi integrations (ALEX DEX, Bitlayer, B² Network, Merlin DEX ~8), data/infra (Hiro, Ord, OrdinalsBot, Geniidata ~6), and miscellaneous (~8)

switching_cost_analysis: Switching costs are very low: BRC-20 tokens are fungible text inscriptions with no lock-in mechanism, wallets (UniSat, Xverse) support all BRC-20 tokens interchangeably, and competing standards (Runes, ARC-20) are supported by the same wallet and marketplace infrastructure, meaning users and developers can migrate to a competing token or standard with a single wallet update and no sunk cost beyond speculative token holdings.
rationale: ORDI holds the #1 market-cap position in the btc-ordinals CoinGecko category (~38% share) and benefits from first-mover brand recognition and the deepest CEX liquidity (~10 top-tier exchange listings), but its moat is structurally weak because BRC-20 is a permissionless, copycat-friendly standard with no protocol revenue capture, no TVL, and near-zero switching costs. Regulatory risk is similar to other BRC-20/inscription tokens: all face the same unresolved questions around whether inscription-based tokens constitute securities, and Bitcoin's base-layer neutrality provides no special regulatory shelter versus competitors. The emergence of the Runes protocol (Casey Rodarmor's own successor standard) is the primary competitive threat, as it has native Bitcoin protocol support and lower on-chain overhead.
sources: https://www.coingecko.com/en/categories/btc-ordinals — category market cap and ORDI market cap (accessed 2025-07-10)
